purpose: Small objective reranking pilot with hidden unit tests and no HH label dependence.

tasks.prompt: Write a Python function `rotate_list(values, k)` that returns a new list rotated to the right by `k` positions. Negative `k` should rotate left. Do not mutate the input list.

Write a Python function `merge_intervals(intervals)` that takes a list of `[start, end]` integer pairs and returns overlapping or touching intervals merged into sorted order. Touching intervals such as `[1, 2]` and `[2, 4]` should merge.

Write a Python function `longest_common_prefix(words)` that returns the longest starting substring shared by every string in the input list. If the list is empty or there is no shared prefix, return an empty string.
Write a Python function `balanced_brackets(text)` that returns `True` if the brackets in the string are balanced and properly nested. Only `()`, `[]`, and `{}` matter; ignore all other characters.

Write a Python function `top_k_frequent(items, k)` that returns the `k` most frequent items from the input list. Break ties by the order in which items first appear in the input. Return fewer than `k` items only if there are not enough unique values.

Write a Python function `chunk_list(items, size)` that splits a list into consecutive chunks of length `size`. The last chunk may be shorter. Raise `ValueError` if `size` is less than or equal to zero.
